thread.title: Judge Meredith Vacca makes history as first woman of color to serve on WDNY bench
thread.title_full: Judge Meredith Vacca makes history as first woman of color to serve on WDNY bench

author: WHAM
title: Judge Meredith Vacca makes history as first woman of color to serve on WDNY bench
text:
Rochester, N.Y. — It was a historic Friday in Western New York, as Judge Meredith Vacca was officially sworn in to serve in the Western District of New York.
Vacca, a Rochester native and Monroe County Court judge, will be the first Korean-American and woman of color to serve on the federal bench.
BACKGROUND: [Monroe County Court Judge Meredith Vacca confirmed as federal judge for WNY](https://13wham.com/news/local/monroe-county-court-judge-meredith-vacca-confirmed-as-federal-judge-for-wny-western-district-new-york-rochester-buffalo-greece-schumer-biden#)
She was nominated to the position by President Joe Biden after being recommended by Sen. Chuck Schumer.
"It is an honor of a lifetime to serve as United States district judge in the Western District of New York," Vacca said after taking the oath of office Friday. "It is a job that I cherish, that I look forward to doing every single day. It is a job that comes with great responsibility, that I take on humbly, earnestly and ardently. And it is a job that has given me the opportunity to hopefully do good for others."
Vacca will now serve a lifetime appointment.
“As a long-time Rochester-area resident and a graduate of the University of Buffalo Law School, Judge Vacca is a true blue Western New Yorker," Schumer said in a statement. "As a Korean-American who was adopted at 6 months old and comes from a family of attorneys, she represents the rich diversity of Monroe County. I was proud to recommend the extraordinarily qualified Judge Vacca to serve and know she will bring talent, experience, and respect for the law to the bench.”
_____
